sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(104) — Owens River (Inyo and Mono Cos.)
sources.quotedText: (104) Owens River (Inyo and Mono Cos.), except (A), (B), (C), and (D) below. All year. 5 trout (A) Upper Owens River from Benton Bridge road crossing upstream to Big Springs. Above Big Springs, see Deadman Creek (subsection (b)(41)). All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (B) Upper Owens River from Benton Bridge road crossing to Crowley Lake. Last Sat. in Apr. through Jul. 31. 5 trout (C) From Pleasant Valley Dam downstream to footbridge at lower end of Pleasant Valley Campground. Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15. 2 trout. 4 trout in possession. Nov. 16 through the Fri. preceding the last Sat. in Apr. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (D) From footbridge at lower end of Pleasant Valley Campground east (downstream) to 5 Bridges Road. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: Fly Fishing Lower Owens River Wild Trout Section — The Ecological Angler
CCR T14 §5.85 — Trout (statewide daily bag and possession limits)
sources.quotedText: daily bag and possession limits mean the total number of trout in combination

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: General rule for the Owens River in Inyo and Mono counties, except the segments described in (A), (B), (C), and (D).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year on the Owens River generally, except the four described sub-segments
regulations.verbatimText: Owens River (Inyo and Mono Cos.), except (A), (B), (C), and (D) below. All year.

parameters.counting_rule: 5 trout daily bag; per CCR §7.50(a), unless otherwise provided it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit, so the possession limit is also 5 trout.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag and possession limit on the general river

parameters.note: Above Big Springs, see Deadman Creek, CCR §7.50(b)(41).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year, catch-and-release, from Benton Bridge road crossing upstream to Big Springs
regulations.verbatimText: Upper Owens River from Benton Bridge road crossing upstream to Big Springs. Above Big Springs, see Deadman Creek (subsection (b)(41)). All year.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, Benton Bridge road crossing upstream to Big Springs
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: 0 trout — catch-and-release only, Benton Bridge road crossing upstream to Big Springs
Open last Saturday in April through July 31, Benton Bridge road crossing to Crowley Lake
regulations.verbatimText: Upper Owens River from Benton Bridge road crossing to Crowley Lake. Last Sat. in Apr. through Jul. 31.

parameters.counting_rule: 5 trout daily bag; per CCR §7.50(a), unless otherwise provided it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit, so the possession limit is also 5 trout.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag and possession limit, Benton Bridge road crossing to Crowley Lake

end.verbatim: Friday preceding the last Saturday in April

regulations.humanSummary: Open last Saturday in April through November 15 (general season), and open catch-and-release November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, from Pleasant Valley Dam to the Pleasant Valley Campground footbridge
regulations.verbatimText: Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15. ... Nov. 16 through the Fri. preceding the last Sat. in Apr.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies during the general season, last Saturday in April through November 15.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 trout daily bag, 4 in possession, last Saturday in April through November 15, Pleasant Valley Dam to the campground footbridge
regulations.verbatimText: Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15. 2 trout. 4 trout in possession.

parameters.note: Applies only during the November 16 through Friday preceding the last Saturday in April catch-and-release period.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April.

regulations.humanSummary: 0 trout — catch-and-release only, November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April
Open all year, catch-and-release, from the Pleasant Valley Campground footbridge downstream to Five Bridges Road
regulations.verbatimText: From footbridge at lower end of Pleasant Valley Campground east (downstream) to 5 Bridges Road. All year.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, campground footbridge downstream to Five Bridges Road
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: 0 trout — catch-and-release only, campground footbridge downstream to Five Bridges Road

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
